behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment about relevance/specificity of the reply to thread details; a stubbed model can always return a fixture that appears specific and hide failures. Rejected Workflow because the behavior text targets a single model-output quality property (the draft content), not multi-step agentic consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real LLM on representative real email threads and assert the draft cites concrete thread-specific points (e.g., named requests, dates, commitments) rather than generic templates.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: This behavior can be validated by wiring draft-claim extraction/checker logic to a real local KB fixture and verifying contradiction flags across module handoff. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require real external contract checks or round-trip external side-effect visibility. Rejected Agentic because the behavior text specifies contradiction checking against KB entries but does not explicitly require model-quality judgment from a real LLM to pass.
behaviors.test_description: Feed contradictory draft statements plus KB records through the real checker pipeline (parser/extractor + KB lookup + contradiction rule) using a local test DB/files and verify flagged outputs and reasons.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on the model’s actual generation behavior under missing-context conditions (whether it chooses to disclaim vs hallucinate). Mocked outputs hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because this is still a single model decision quality check, not a multi-agentic sequence requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Using real LLM calls and retrieval results intentionally returning no relevant KB entries, assert the produced draft contains an uncertainty/disclaimer statement and does not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving absence/presence of an externally visible send side effect at the email boundary (round-trip observation: not sent before approval, sent after approval). In-process mocks can mask transport/queue/integration behavior. Rejected Workflow because behavior text is about gating a side effect, not multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the real send path against a real/staging mail transport: create draft, do not approve, verify via outbox/provider API that no message was sent; then approve and verify exactly one sent message appears.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full agent pipeline check with cross-step handoffs where model-produced draft content flows into fact-checking and approval presentation. Validating only isolated agentic output would miss sequencing/state-transfer failures. Rejected lower levels because the behavior text explicitly requires real end-to-end sequence execution for a real thread.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario on a real email thread through all stages (ingest -> LLM draft -> fact check -> approval UI/state), asserting each stage output is consumed by the next and final state is correct before/after approval action.
